about.title: About the Project
about.body:
The Žižkov Tunnel Association is an independent initiative founded in 2025 by prominent figures from the fields of architecture, art, and culture. Our goal is to transform the Žižkov Tunnel into a vibrant gallery of contemporary art and an open public space.

The project connects the districts of Žižkov, Karlín, and Holešovice, forming a cultural axis through the heart of Prague. We draw inspiration from world-renowned projects such as the High Line in New York City.

The association is built on principles of openness, collaboration, and long-term care for public space.

list.name: Martin Wichterle
list.role: Industrialist, owner of the Wikov engineering group and the Bomma and Rückl glassworks

list.name: Jiří Řezák
list.role: Architect and owner of the QARTA Architektura studio

list.name: Jiří Fajt
list.role: Art historian and artistic director, former Director General of the National Gallery in Prague

findUs.title: How to Find Us
findUs.address:
Žižkov Tunnel
Prague 3 — Žižkov / Karlín / Holešovice
findUs.entranceZizkov: Entrance from Žižkov: Seifertova street, near Náměstí Winstona Churchilla.
findUs.transport: Public transport: Metro B Florenc, tram Tachovské náměstí or Lipanská.
findUs.mapNote: The tunnel is open daily to pedestrians and cyclists.

meta.title: Žižkov Tunnel — Association
meta.description: Transforming the Žižkov Tunnel into a world-class art gallery. A project connecting Žižkov, Karlín and Holešovice — the cultural axis of Prague.
